author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods
book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods

title: Canon XIV.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: It is decreed that among the clergy, presbyters and deacons who abstain from flesh shall taste of it, and afterwards, if they shall so please, may abstain.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: But if they disdain it, and will not even eat herbs served with flesh, but disobey the canon, let them be removed from their order.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: A priest who is an abstainer from flesh, let him merely taste it and so let him abstain.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: But if he will not taste even the vegetables cooked with the meat let him be deposed (πεπάυσθω).

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: There is a serious dispute about the reading of the Greek text.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: I have followed Routh, who, relying on three mss. the Collectio of John of Antioch and the Latin versions, reads εἰ δὲ βδελύσσοιντο instead of the εἰ δὲ βούλοιντο of the ordinary text, which as Bp.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Beveridge had pointed out before has no meaning unless a μὴ be introduced.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Zonaras points out that the canon chiefly refers to the Love feasts.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: I cannot agree with Hefele in his translation of the last clause.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: He makes the reference to “this present canon,” I think it is clearly to the 53 (52) of the so-called Canons of the Apostles, τῷ κανόνι “the well-known Canon.”
